symptoms.symptom_name: Adrenergic surge — drenching diaphoresis, pallor, fine tremor, palpitations and intense hunger

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Autonomic counter-regulatory response to falling glucose; a hallmark early feature that precedes neuroglycopenia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Neuroglycopenia — confusion, bizarre behaviour, blank staring, drowsiness and inability to follow commands or swallow

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Neuroglycopenic dysfunction marks severe hypoglycaemia and airway risk; defines the emergency and precludes oral treatment.
Altered mentation and visual change can occur in stroke, but hypoglycaemia is a reversible mimic that must be excluded/corrected first.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bedside capillary blood glucose 2.1 mmol/L (clinician-measured; no home glucometer available)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A critically low bedside glucose with concordant neuroglycopenic symptoms confirms hypoglycaemia; here it is a sealed clinician measurement because the household had no glucose meter — the AI Doctor infers severity from the reported obtundation, not a measured number.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recurrent fasting- and exertion-provoked hypoglycaemic spells over three months, relieved by eating (Whipple pattern)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fasting/exertional lows that resolve with carbohydrate, recurring over months, are the classic history of autonomous endogenous hyperinsulinism.

symptoms.symptom_name: No diabetes and no access to exogenous insulin or insulin secretagogue

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of exogenous insulin/secretagogue points the hyperinsulinism to an endogenous (autonomous) source rather than an iatrogenic or factitious one.
No reported access to insulin or sulfonylurea makes surreptitious use less likely, though definitive exclusion needs C-peptide/proinsulin and a drug screen during a supervised fast.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recent unintentional weight gain from constant defensive eating (including overnight snacking)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Defensive eating to pre-empt lows, with consequent weight gain, is characteristic of insulinoma.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of cutaneous hyperpigmentation and normal baseline blood pressure

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No hyperpigmentation, no postural hypotension and no characteristic electrolyte pattern on the clinician's assessment reduce the likelihood of an adrenal cause — a clinician-observed sign, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Fine resting tremor and sinus tachycardia on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective adrenergic signs corroborate the counter-regulatory response — a clinician measurement, not available over chat; the AI infers the adrenergic state from the carer's report of sweating and trembling.

symptoms.symptom_name: Anticipated inappropriately elevated plasma insulin and C-peptide during a supervised fast

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Detectable insulin and C-peptide at the time of documented hypoglycaemia during a supervised 72-hour fast confirms endogenous hyperinsulinism — the confirmatory limb of the work-up, a hospital/endocrine investigation never available in telehealth in real time.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Whipple triad of endogenous hyperinsulinaemic hypoglycaemia
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Symptoms of hypoglycaemia + a documented low plasma/bedside glucose + relief with carbohydrate, recurring with fasting in a non-diabetic — Whipple's triad, the decisive pattern that mandates an endocrine work-up for insulinoma.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Immediate blood glucose measurement and correction (ambulance/ED point-of-care and laboratory glucose)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms and treats the critical low; not obtainable via telehealth here because no home glucometer is available, so emergency services must both measure and correct it.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Supervised 72-hour fast with paired plasma glucose, insulin, C-peptide, proinsulin and sulfonylurea/meglitinide screen
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The definitive biochemical work-up for endogenous hyperinsulinism / insulinoma and to exclude surreptitious secretagogue use — an inpatient/endocrine investigation, not a telehealth action.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Pancreatic localisation imaging (CT/MRI/endoscopic ultrasound) after biochemical confirmation
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Localises a suspected insulinoma to plan curative surgery; sequenced after the fasting study, in specialist care.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent non-contrast neuroimaging for stroke as the first step
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Do not pursue a stroke/CNS-infection pathway first — correct the documented hypoglycaemia; imaging is only needed if deficits persist after glucose normalises.
